jokes.text: I just got this sudden urge. / Uh-oh, you're doing it again.
jokes.setup: Elaine claims painting is just a sudden urge
jokes.punchline: Alex implies this is a pattern, setting up the reveal
jokes.explanation: Good setup that pays off immediately with the paint layers joke

jokes.text: Do you realize that every time you break up with a guy you paint your apartment?
jokes.setup: Friends offering to help paint Elaine's apartment
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is a recurring pattern tied to breakups
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based observation that reveals Elaine's coping mechanism with precision and believability

jokes.text: Elaine, there must be 20 coats of paint here. It's been a rough year.
jokes.setup: The painting-after-breakups revelation
jokes.punchline: The understatement of 'rough year' for 20 breakups
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and deadpan delivery that amplifies the absurdity

jokes.text: Let's see, the blue one is Tom, and under Tom was Roger, and under Roger was Eric, and under Eric was... I forget. Looks like a dull gray. My ex-husband.
jokes.setup: Cataloguing paint layers as past relationships
jokes.punchline: The ex-husband being characterized as 'dull gray' at the bottom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor that builds tension then delivers a perfect character reveal about the ex-husband

jokes.text: Once in, uh, '65, some friends of mine and I painted my place by dipping our heads in buckets and then painting with our hair.
jokes.setup: Offering to help with painting technique
jokes.punchline: The completely impractical and absurd hair-painting method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim Ignatowski absurdist logic delivered with complete sincerity - quintessential character comedy

jokes.text: We were thinking of using brushes and rollers.
jokes.setup: Jim's absurd hair-painting suggestion
jokes.punchline: The perfectly reasonable counter-suggestion delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Strong straight-man response that grounds the absurdity

jokes.text: Hey, wait a minute, wait a minute, you know, this is silly, trying to do this and drink beer at the same time. You're right.
jokes.setup: Appears they're going to focus on work
jokes.punchline: They stop working to focus on drinking
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that reveals their true priorities

jokes.text: That's all right, as long as we don't put the sound on, we'll watch it every once in a while.
jokes.setup: Trying to justify watching TV while painting
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous compromise of silent TV watching
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing self-deception about productivity

jokes.text: I got two models... The one-buck model and the ten-buck. What's the difference? The ten-buck takes me longer to pick.
jokes.setup: Louie offering padlock options for lockers
jokes.punchline: Admitting both are equally ineffective against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie - corrupt but honest about his corruption

jokes.text: Because the whole thing is just so stupid and corny and high school. And worst of all, it is getting me hot.
jokes.setup: Elaine criticizing the romantic poems
jokes.punchline: Admitting they're working on her despite her intellectual objections
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Elaine's intellect vs. emotions

jokes.text: I once wrote a poem for my wife. She graded it.
jokes.setup: Alex denying he's much of a poet
jokes.punchline: The humbling detail about his wife grading his poem
jokes.explanation: Economical joke that paints a perfect picture of married life

jokes.text: You could have taken some time with that.
jokes.setup: Elaine's immediate shock at Louie claiming authorship
jokes.punchline: Louie's wounded reaction to her instant dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Louie's surprising vulnerability

jokes.text: Cascading... cascades... cascading water... a waterfall... clouds... lots of them, white and puffy. You know, clouds... And flowers covered with dew, and trees hanging over, and you and me naked on a rock.
jokes.setup: Louie attempting to recite romantic poetry
jokes.punchline: His crude, fumbling attempt ending with naked imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - trying to be romantic but revealing his true nature

jokes.text: That is the most disgusting poetry. Just get away from me.
jokes.setup: Louie's crude 'poetry'
jokes.punchline: Elaine's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Necessary reaction but not particularly clever on its own

jokes.text: This close to the promised land.
jokes.setup: Elaine rejecting Louie's advances completely
jokes.punchline: Louie's delusional assessment that he was 'close'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-delusion from Louie - shows his complete disconnection from reality

jokes.text: I don't work weekends. You been gone nine days. Right. Jim. Weekends are only two days. Oh, I thought we'd switched to the metric system.
jokes.setup: Jim's been absent for nine days claiming weekend off
jokes.punchline: His metric system non-sequitur explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim logic - completely nonsensical but delivered with absolute conviction

jokes.text: Yeah, if you consider dark, wavy hair, magnetic eyes and a killer smile attractive. Some women go for that.
jokes.setup: Describing Don Reavey as good-looking
jokes.punchline: The massive understatement after listing his attractive features
jokes.explanation: Nice sarcastic delivery showing Elaine's conflicted feelings

jokes.text: Maybe he writes better than he looks, talks... is.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the poet might stay anonymous
jokes.punchline: The escalating criticism ending with his entire existence
jokes.explanation: Great Jim moment - accidentally brutal honesty delivered innocently

jokes.text: Sure. Is this you, Alex?
jokes.setup: Alex asking if Jim is the poet
jokes.punchline: Jim's equally confused counter-question
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim confusion - shows his unique thought process

jokes.text: Couldn't find a poem? I couldn't find a library.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining he couldn't find a poem at the library
jokes.punchline: Revealing the real problem was finding the library itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim logic - escalates to the absurd in a completely believable way for his character

jokes.text: She only wants to know who wrote the poems because she doesn't know who wrote the poems.
jokes.setup: Alex asking why Jim won't tell Elaine
jokes.punchline: Jim's circular, meaningless logic
jokes.explanation: Typical Jim non-logic delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: She only wants to know who wrote the poems because she doesn't know who wrote the poems.
jokes.setup: Alex asks why Jim won't reveal himself
jokes.punchline: Jim's circular logic explanation
jokes.explanation: Typical Jim twisted logic that makes no sense but sounds profound

jokes.text: There are a lot of people in this world who think I'm a flake. Who thinks that? Me, for one.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining people think he's a flake
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's the primary person who thinks this
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - shows Jim's unexpected self-awareness

jokes.text: Let's shake on it. We just shook on it. Oh, good.
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting they shake hands on their agreement
jokes.punchline: His satisfaction despite Alex pointing out they already did
jokes.explanation: Simple Jim moment showing his scatterbrained nature

jokes.text: Can you believe she fell for that? Hey, I fell for it, and I wrote them.
jokes.setup: Don bragging about deceiving Elaine
jokes.punchline: Jim's innocent admission of his own gullibility
jokes.explanation: Beautiful irony - Jim's innocence contrasted with Don's cynicism

jokes.text: Y-You mean your head doesn't hurt and your tongue doesn't feel thick and grungy, and-and your body doesn't feel like it's been turned inside out? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Describing hangover symptoms to Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual 'yeah' to having all these symptoms while feeling 'fine'
jokes.explanation: Good Jim moment - his altered perception of normal vs. abnormal

jokes.text: Jim, some people find that feeling very uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Jim's casual acceptance of severe hangover symptoms
jokes.punchline: Alex's massive understatement about discomfort
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response to Jim's absurdity

jokes.text: When I was with people, I was the life of the party, but I'd get home alone, all by myself, I'd clam up.
jokes.setup: Jim describing having a split personality
jokes.punchline: The redundancy of 'alone, all by myself' and the irony of clamming up when alone
jokes.explanation: Jim's typical redundant phrasing and backwards logic

jokes.text: They're going away for the whole weekend. That's pretty serious... The two of them alone together for nine days.
jokes.setup: Tony mentioning a weekend trip
jokes.punchline: Jim's math making a weekend into nine days
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Jim's earlier weekend confusion

jokes.text: Um, Pepe, Carlo, Pedro, uh, Pavlovsky, Carlos, uh, uh, Jeff, Ahmad... Latka. No. The bald guy who comes on Thursdays? Sheila? Sheila?
jokes.setup: Elaine desperately guessing who wrote the poems
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly random guesses including women's names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Elaine's desperation and confusion

jokes.text: Well, that's everybody in the garage. So I guess it's nobody. / Yeah... it's nobody.
jokes.setup: Elaine concludes it must be nobody from the garage
jokes.punchline: Alex agrees while obviously lying
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment where audience knows more than Elaine

jokes.text: I know a pill that would do it. But it has a terrible side effect. Like what? It wears off.
jokes.setup: Jim offering a solution to make Elaine feel better
jokes.punchline: The 'side effect' being that it's temporary like all medicine
jokes.explanation: Clever Jim observation about the nature of temporary solutions

jokes.text: I was, uh, going to buy you a scarf, but I couldn't find a department store.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his gift choice after building a castle
jokes.punchline: His typical inability to find obvious locations
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Jim's consistent navigation problems

jokes.text: I was, uh, going to buy you a scarf, but I couldn't find a department store.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking about his thoughtful gesture
jokes.punchline: Another example of Jim's navigation problems
jokes.explanation: Good callback reinforcing Jim's character while maintaining the sweet moment

jokes.text: Hey, uh, I didn't have time for a moat.
jokes.setup: Elaine questioning something about the castle
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual mention of not including a moat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim - treating impossible things as minor inconveniences

jokes.text: Well, uh... your castle has about 150,000 miles on it.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking why he didn't drive his van
jokes.punchline: Revealing the 'castle' is actually his van
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that recontextualizes the entire romantic gesture - the 'castle' was his beat-up van

jokes.text: Hey, how about, uh, I crash here for a year or so?
jokes.setup: Jim's van/castle being revealed as transportation
jokes.punchline: His casual suggestion to move in for 'a year or so'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Jim's complete lack of boundaries

jokes.text: But you are the dearest, sweetest, most wonderful person. / I think you're right.
jokes.setup: Elaine's heartfelt compliment to Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with Jim's innocent ego showing through

jokes.text: Funny thing, there used to be a time when that was enough.
jokes.setup: Elaine calling him wonderful but not romantic
jokes.punchline: Jim's wistful observation about changing times
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly poignant moment showing Jim's depth beneath the comedy
